display_name: An IPv6 neighbor stuck INCOMPLETE does not always mean the host is down — check VLAN, ICMPv6 ACLs, and solicited-node multicast
context: An IPv6 neighbor entry is INCOMPLETE and the operator cannot reach the host from the device
observation: 'show ipv6 neighbor' / 'show ndp' shows the target IPv6 in INCOMPLETE with no link-layer address; pings to it time out
naive_interpretation: The host is powered off or its IPv6 stack is down and not answering Neighbor Solicitations
correct_interpretation: INCOMPLETE means the device multicast a Neighbor Solicitation to the target's solicited-node address and received no Neighbor Advertisement. A dead host is one cause, but several network-side causes produce the identical symptom: (1) the host is in a different VLAN than the device's SVI, so the NS never reaches it; (2) an ACL or CoPP policy is dropping ICMPv6 NS/NA (types 135/136) — remember ND is ICMPv6; (3) the solicited-node multicast (ff02::1:ffXX:XXXX) is not being delivered because MLD-snooping pruned it or the node never joined the group; (4) the device has no valid link-local to source the NS from; (5) on SONiC the kernel resolved the neighbor but orchagent failed to program the ASIC neighbor entry, so software looks fine while hardware drops. Always confirm Layer 2 reachability and that ICMPv6 ND is permitted before concluding the host is down.
why: INCOMPLETE only proves no NA was received — it does not distinguish a dead host from an NS that never arrived
ND is multicast ICMPv6 to a solicited-node group, so VLAN mismatch, ICMPv6 ACLs, or MLD-snooping pruning all suppress resolution while the host is perfectly alive
On SONiC the software neighbor cache and the ASIC neighbor table can disagree, so a kernel-resolved neighbor can still be dropped in hardware

confirmation_commands.purpose: Confirm INCOMPLETE/no MAC, and which interface/VLAN the NS is sent on
Verify INCMP state and the expected egress interface
Check INCMP vs REACH/STALE and the interface
Kernel state INCOMPLETE/FAILED for the address
If kernel resolved but this is empty, the failure is ASIC programming, not the host

traps: Blaming the host/NIC when the real problem is a VLAN mismatch or an ICMPv6 ACL
Forgetting ND is ICMPv6 — an ICMPv6 deny produces INCOMPLETE entries
Overlooking MLD-snooping pruning the solicited-node multicast that NS depends on
On SONiC, trusting 'show ndp' alone without checking the ASIC neighbor table
Not verifying the device has a valid link-local to source the NS

sources.title: RFC 4861 — Neighbor Discovery for IPv6 (neighbor cache states 7.3.2)
